To fix the insecure deserialization vulnerability in the provided Java code snippet, you should implement proper security measures to ensure that only safe and expected objects are deserialized. Here are some steps you can take to secure the code:

1. Use a whitelist approach: Instead of deserializing any object provided by the user input, maintain a whitelist of allowed classes that can be deserialized. Only deserialize objects of those whitelisted classes.

2. Implement input validation: Validate the user input before attempting deserialization. Ensure that the input meets certain criteria or constraints before proceeding with deserialization.

3. Use a secure ObjectInputStream: Customize and extend the ObjectInputStream class to override the resolveClass method and validate the class being deserialized against your whitelist.

4. Implement digital signatures or checksums: Sign or include checksums in serialized objects to verify their integrity before deserialization.
